journal_entry.description: Short-term loan advanced to business partner, one-year term at 4.5% annual interest
journal_entry.rationale: The loan principal is recorded as a short-term loan receivable (asset increase), as the term is one year. The funds are transferred from Target's chequing account (asset decrease). This entry records only the principal disbursement; monthly interest revenue will be recorded separately as earned per the accrual basis under IFRS. Loans are not subject to HST/GST as financial services are exempt from taxation in Canada.

output.reason: Cash transferred from Target's chequing account to the partner = asset decrease (cash leaving). The loan receivable is recorded on the debit side (asset increase), but we only classify credits. The monthly interest receivable terms affect future revenue recognition but do not create a credit line in this initial loan advance transaction.

credit_classifier.reason: Cash transferred from Target's chequing account to the partner = asset decrease (cash leaving). The loan receivable is recorded on the debit side (asset increase), but we only classify credits. The monthly interest receivable terms affect future revenue recognition but do not create a credit line in this initial loan advance transaction.

output.reason: Target advances a short-term loan of $2,000,000. On the debit side, this creates a "Loans Receivable" or "Notes Receivable" asset account (an amount owed to Target by the business partner). This is 1 asset increase. The cash transfer out and the interest receivable setup are handled on the credit side or in future entries, not on the debit side of this initial loan advance transaction.

debit_classifier.reason: Target advances a short-term loan of $2,000,000. The cash transfer out and the interest receivable setup are handled on the credit side or in future entries, not on the debit side of this initial loan advance transaction.
